assertions.title: Personalization slider → re-render

steps.action: Open dashboard and expand personalization panel
steps.observed: 6 dimension weight sliders (Geopolitical, Infrastructure, Economic, Security, Climate, Technology) all at 50, plus 3 toggles (Agency Bias, Critical Thinking, Federation)

steps.action: Note current gauge/clock values in η lane
steps.observed: η lane gauges all show 0.0 THREAT (geopolitical radars have no score range data); Π lane Integration Test Radar shows 2.0 THREAT

steps.action: Adjust Geopolitical weight slider from 50 to 90
Check for API call in network log
steps.expected: API call triggered with new weight value
steps.observed: Regular polling GET /api/radars calls continue (~12s interval), but NO dedicated API call triggered by slider change. Weights are client-side only (localStorage).

steps.expected: Gauge/clock values change to reflect adjusted weights
steps.observed: Gauge values remain unchanged at 0.0 THREAT. The weight application code exists in EtaLane.tsx (factor = weight/50 applied to score range medians) but all η lane radars have no deterministicSnapshot.score_ranges data, so the factor multiplies 0 and produces no visible change.

evidence.network: GET /api/radars polling every ~12s (not triggered by slider change)

assertions.issues: Gauge/clock values do not change when adjusting personalization sliders because: (1) The slider change is saved to localStorage only, not sent as an API call. (2) The weight application code in EtaLane.tsx requires non-zero score_ranges in deterministicSnapshot, but all η lane radars have empty score ranges. (3) The main threat gauge (0.0) does not incorporate personalization weights. Step 3 (observe API call) fails because the weight change is purely client-side. Step 4 (gauge values change) fails because 0 × any_factor = 0.

assertions.title: Polling auto-update: New signal reflected without refresh

steps.action: Open dashboard and note current state
steps.observed: Dashboard shows η lane with VAL PROC-005 (0.0 THREAT) and VAL PROC-004 (0.0 THREAT), Π lane with Integration Test Radar (2.0 THREAT)

steps.action: Create new radar 'Polling Test Cross007' via MCP (radar_create, category: geopolitical)
steps.observed: Radar created with id 000c9017-027e-4c31-8975-65e30f2094c1, slug polling-test-cross007

steps.action: Submit assessment packet via MCP (radar_submit_packet with transit_flow:4, attack_tempo:3, insurance_availability:2)

steps.expected: Live snapshot generated with signal data
steps.observed: Live snapshot generated: transit_flow median=4, attack_tempo median=3, insurance_availability median=2

steps.action: Wait ≤15s and check dashboard without refresh
steps.expected: New radar appears with updated gauge values
steps.observed: After 15s: 'Polling Test Cross007' appeared in η lane with 3.0 THREAT gauge, signal dimensions visible (transit_flow: 4, attack_tempo: 3, insurance_availability: 2). No manual refresh performed.

evidence.network: Periodic GET /api/radars returning updated data with new radar tile

steps.action: Open dashboard and note current state
steps.observed: Dashboard shows Polling Test Cross007 in η lane (3.0 THREAT), Integration Test Radar in Π lane (2.0 THREAT)

steps.action: Create new radar 'MCP Display Test Cross008' via MCP (radar_create, category: test)
steps.observed: Radar created: id f42fc1e2-d74d-4249-ba20-d2bc79c74388, slug mcp-display-cross008, category test, status active
After 15s: 'MCP Display Test Cross008' appeared in Π (Connections) lane with ACTIVE badge, 0.0 THREAT gauge, and metadata (0 packets, 0 sources, 0 models). No manual refresh.

evidence.network: MCP POST /mcp tools/call radar_create -> 200; polling GET /api/radars picked up new radar

steps.action: Confirm dashboard loads normally with working API
steps.expected: Dashboard renders with data, no errors
steps.observed: Dashboard fully functional with radar tiles, gauges, and all 3 lanes populated

steps.action: Stop API server (kill process on port 9001)
steps.observed: API server killed. curl to health endpoint fails (connection refused)
Red/amber error banner appears at top: 'Unable to reach the radar API' with 'Retry' button. Radar tiles disappear, 3 lanes remain with placeholders. Critical Thinking section still visible.

steps.action: Check console for uncaught JS errors
steps.observed: Zero uncaught JS errors in console
API restarted successfully: health endpoint returns ok, listening on 9001

steps.action: Verify dashboard recovers after API restart
steps.expected: Dashboard recovers and shows data again
steps.observed: After next polling cycle: error banner gone, all radar tiles restored, data displayed normally

evidence.network: GET /api/radars fails with connection refused when API down; resumes normally after restart

frictions.description: MCP Streamable HTTP transport requires session management (mcp-session-id header from initialize response). Each curl call needs to maintain the session or tool calls fail with 'Server not initialized'. Not mentioned in user-testing.md.
frictions.resolution: Extracted mcp-session-id from initialize response headers and passed it with subsequent requests

frictions.description: radar_submit_packet expects a 'packet' wrapper object with specific required fields (thread_id, radar_id, module_version_id, timestamp_utc, model_id, signal_scores with value/range/confidence/reason), not the simplified format shown in the task prompt
frictions.resolution: Used tools/list to discover correct schema, then submitted properly formatted packet

frictions.description: Submitting a packet does not automatically trigger live snapshot reduction. A separate radar_reduce_live call is needed for signal data to appear in the API response for dashboard polling

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-CROSS-007, VAL-CROSS-008, VAL-CROSS-009), 1 failed (VAL-CROSS-003: personalization slider saves weights to localStorage and code applies them via factor multiplication, but no score_ranges data exists in test radars for visible gauge changes, and no API call is triggered by slider changes).
